_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Carrier Emissions Data Collection Infrastructure**
Scope: The workspace must establish or upgrade a systematic process to collect, store, and validate ocean carrier CII ratings and well-to-wake emission factor documentation for every ocean lane used. This applies across the Americas/Europe/Asia trade lanes. Deadline: Operational before any NZF-mandated GFI surcharges materialize (~2027–2028); immediate value for existing shipper Scope 3 reporting needs. Gap from baseline: Most freight forwarder TMS and rate management systems do not natively ingest CII ratings or LCA-compliant WtW factors — a custom data field or integration layer is required.

**2. Fuel Price and Surcharge Monitoring Feed**
Scope: The FFT portal provides live data from over 22 providers on alternative marine fuel prices (LNG, methanol, ammonia, biofuel, hydrogen). The workspace should establish a monitoring workflow — at minimum quarterly, ideally monthly — that ingests FFT portal fuel price data and maps it to ocean carrier surcharge exposure by lane. This directly affects the high-value, time-sensitive cargo verticals (live events, fine art, film/TV, humanitarian) where freight cost predictability is critical. Deadline: Immediate; portal data is live and publicly accessible. Gap from baseline: Typically absent from forwarder rate-desk workflows.

**3. Carrier Contract Review and Green Clause Audit**
Scope: All active ocean carrier service contracts and spot rate agreements should be reviewed for: (a) existence and scope of environmental/GHG surcharge clauses; (b) regulatory-change pass-through provisions; (c) language referencing MARPOL Annex VI compliance costs. As the NZF moves toward adoption, carriers will seek to pass GFI deficit unit costs downstream. Deadline: Within 60 days and upon any NZF adoption event. Gap from baseline: Most legacy forwarder agreements predate GFI pricing structures.

**4. Shipper Scope 3 Reporting Capability**
Scope: The workspace must be capable of providing shipper clients with per-shipment or per-lane ocean emission estimates using WtW methodology consistent with IMO LCA guidelines. This is directly material to clients in luxury goods, fine art, film/TV, and automotive who face CSRD or investor ESG reporting obligations. Training of operations and account management staff on WtW vs. TTW distinction and correct emission factor sourcing is required. Deadline: Aligned with shipper reporting cycles; CSRD phase-in for large EU-linked clients accelerates urgency. Gap from baseline: Many forwarders still use TTW (tank-to-wake only) factors or legacy GLEC Framework defaults that may not align with IMO LCA guidelines.

**5. Alternative Fuel Bunkering and Port Infrastructure Awareness**
Scope: The FFT portal's Innovation section and green shipping corridor tracker (ZESM) provide updated maps of port bunkering infrastructure for zero/near-zero fuels. The workspace's procurement and routing teams should integrate port-level alternative fuel availability data into carrier selection for clients with green shipping commitments. This is especially relevant for humanitarian cargo routing through SIDS-connected ports, where infrastructure gaps may persist longest. Deadline: Ongoing; update port assessments at least semi-annually. Gap from baseline: Typically not part of standard carrier/port selection criteria.

**6. Capacity-Building and Staff Training**
Scope: The FFT portal hosts e-learning resources including the GHG SMART Training Programme delivered via IMO LMS Moodle and the CrewWIND online course. The workspace should assign relevant staff (sustainability, operations, business development) to complete available IMO e-learning modules on alternative marine fuels and GHG regulation. This supports credible advisory service to shipper clients and informed carrier negotiations. Deadline: Within 90 days. Gap from baseline: IMO e-learning is free and publicly accessible but rarely utilized by forwarder operations teams.

*Source: IMO Future Fuels and Technology portal, futurefuels.imo.org, launched April 2024; IMO FFT Project page, https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/future-fuels-and-technology.aspx; IMO e-learning page, https://futurefuels.imo.org/learning-resources/e-learning-and-training-packages/.*
